title: 💼 Professional Experience
text:
AEOP Fellowship (Remote) Jun 2025 – Sept 2025 🏢 US ARMY DEVCOM Chemical Biological Center, USA 📝 Develop innovative methods to improve the performance of detection dogs.


Summer Intern (Part-time) Jun 2025 – Aug 2025 🏢 State University of New York, NY, USA - 🤖 Developed framework for horizon scanning. - 🧠 Enhanced white papers with new research from the 64 SUNY campuses. - 📈 Built blog post communications pipeline.


Research Coordinator  Jun 2025 – Aug 2025 🏢 State University of New York at Cobleskill, NY, USA - 🖼️ Coordinated Round Goby detection dog project with USGS. - 🚀 Collected samples, trained participants, ran expiremental trials.

title: Katie B
text:
Welcome to my profile! 





Hello, I’m Katie Baronowski  🎓 PhD Student in Information Science 🏫 State University of New York at Albany (UAlbany) 📍 New York, USA 
🔬 Research Interests:
- Information-driven algorithms - Odor-source localization - Detection K9-technology interaction
Keywords: odor source localization; information-driven search algorithms; mobile sensing; detection dogs; UAS; spatial data science; applied AI; sensor fusion; human-animal-computer interaction

title: Katie B
text:
Education
🎓 PhD Student in Information Science, State University of New York at Albany, USA (Aug 2024 – Present)  - Primary Specialization: Data Analytics - Secondary Specialization: TBD
🎓 Bachelor of Technology in Canine Training and Management and Bachelor of Science in Applied Psychology, SUNY Cobleskill, USA (Aug 2020 – May 2024)  - Outstanding Student in Psychology 2024.  - Student Leadership Award 2024.  - Honors Capstone: Canine discrimination of the enantiomers of carvone.

title: Katie B
text:
Research Overview
My research explores the intersection of Technological and the Biological, developing AI algorithms that enhance predication of target odor and the efficacy of search and rescue.

title: Katie B
text:
Updates
🏆 Received IFW New Frontiers Award (2026).  ✍️ Round goby detection dogs paper in R&R in Biological Invasions (2026).  🚀 Received AEOP Fellowship (2025). 🏆 Received IFW Women in Technology Award (2025).  📜 Attending INTERSECT bootcamp at Princeton New Jersey (2025).  ✍️ Submitted 1st manuscript and as the first author (2025). 🚀 Mentoring undergraduate students in the Mobile Sensor Lab at UAlbany (ongoing).

title: 🔬 Research
text:
Information Science PhD student, with a focus on human-computer interaction, information behavior, and utilizing technology to enhance detection K9-handler teams.

🥼 My work explores: - 🐶 Detection dogs - ℹ️ Information Behavior - 🧑‍💻 Human-Computer Interaction
to improve detection capabilites and response during emergencies and disasters.
